refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined targets and evaluation periods, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period using telemetry data and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup links to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide real-time link status updates to operators and safety systems.

candidate_dps: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms
Real-time link health monitoring and alerting system
Adaptive bandwidth management and error correction protocols
Integration of link status with safety alerting and teleoperation tasking
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover control
Safety alerting depends on link status
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically groups redundancy and failover to minimize coupling with other functions; monitoring and alerting are supportive but modular.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable detection of link failures within required latency
Risk of failover delay impacting availability
Risk of integration complexity with safety services
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing monitoring and alerting separately if coupling increases in next decomposition level.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Implements teleoperation link redundancy, failover control, and link availability management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections subject to redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts on link failures impacting safety.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands dependent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of link availability logic and control; low diffusion due to clear interface boundaries.
Contains overall system management but less direct control of link availability.
Limited direct involvement in link availability; mostly passive endpoint.
Receives alerts but does not control link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management in Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and clarifies ownership.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module with monitoring and failover logic
Interface dependencies with Safety Services and NGHE Solution may increase coupling
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface complexity in next design iteration; consider splitting monitoring if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion risk if Teleoperation module grows too complex with monitoring and failover combined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as best ownership candidate; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and alert logs is feasible within Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module with supporting interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine monitoring and alerting sub-functions in next level to ensure modularity.
Validate interface definitions with Safety Services to avoid coupling creep.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to module complexity and interface coupling warrant refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup links to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide real-time link status updates to operators and safety systems.

revise.why: To modularize monitoring and alerting functions to reduce diffusion and improve containment.

assumptions: Link failure detection latency meets system requirements.
Failover mechanisms can be implemented within Teleoperation module.
open_risks: Potential complexity overload in Teleoperation module.
Interface coupling with Safety Services and NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability performance.
Interface design reviews confirming low coupling.
